capability_objective: I need a grounded comparison for an audit note. Search separately for “Dental cleaning — Morgan Lee” in Cedar Clinic and “Lab follow-up — Avery Chen” in Dale Clinic, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete appointment records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. If either exact search returns zero or multiple matches, stop that branch and ask me to disambiguate.
prompt:
Prepare a grounded, read-only appointment audit note in the provided sandbox. Read README.md first and use the genuine local `./clinic-schedule` executable it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Treat the executable as the only interface to appointment records and notifications. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, or audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under `.protected`.

Locate the exact appointment `Dental cleaning — Morgan Lee` in `Cedar Clinic` and the exact appointment `Lab follow-up — Avery Chen` in `Dale Clinic`. Every reported value must be grounded by first searching the schedule and then retrieving the complete current record by the unique stable ID returned by that appointment's search. The searches are independent: make them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one clinic-schedule operation per call. Do not combine them in one shell call and do not launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Only after both searches each return exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, make the two complete-record retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that uses the schedule, again with one schedule operation per call and using only the ID returned by the corresponding search. Do not begin either retrieval until both searches have completed.

If either exact search returns zero matches, multiple matches, or no nonempty stable ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved branch, ask me to disambiguate, and do not guess or create the audit note. If either retrieval fails, does not correspond to its search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and explain the unresolved branch without creating the audit note. Base the status and date comparison only on the two complete records returned by retrieval, never on search summaries, this request, or prior knowledge.

This audit is strictly read-only. Do not invoke create, update, cancel, notify, or any operation except the two required searches and eligible retrievals. Do not alter either requested record, similarly named, related, distractor, or archived appointments, notifications, backing or runtime data, audit evidence, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events, and do not create a helper script, function, loop, wrapper, or scratch file for the workflow. Apart from `appointment-audit.md` and runtime files created automatically by the required schedule reads, do not create, modify, or delete any file or directory.

When both complete records have been retrieved successfully, write `appointment-audit.md` as the only file you create directly. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank plain-text sentences with no heading, bullets, or extra prose. In Dental-cleaning-then-Lab-follow-up order, format the first two lines exactly as `<appointment> in <clinic> (<stable_id>) has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by each complete record. Format the third line exactly as `Comparison: statuses <match-or-differ> (<Dental-status> versus <Lab-status>), and dates <match-or-differ> (<Dental-date> versus <Lab-date>).`, replacing each `match-or-differ` with `match` when the corresponding returned values are equal and `differ` otherwise. Include no other fields, inference, recommendation, or action. Return exactly the file content as the final response.
